title: pavonia paludicola
intro: Pavonia paludicola, also known as swamp hibiscus or marsh mallow, is a plant species native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Pavonia paludicola include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation and pain, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction or infusion to make teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
